message:
fix(share): split CREATE TABLE AS SELECT into explicit CREATE+INSERT

frankensqlite has a quirk where `CREATE TABLE x AS SELECT ...` returns
Ok but does NOT register the new table in sqlite_master when the
underlying SELECT contains the combination of (a) a correlated subquery
in the SELECT list and (b) a nested derived table in the FROM clause.
The next DDL statement (CREATE INDEX) then fails with "no such table",
which surfaces as the misleading

    message_overview_mv index create failed for "CREATE INDEX
    idx_msg_overview_created ON message_overview_mv(created_ts DESC)":
    Query error: no such table: message_overview_mv

Sidestep it by creating the table with explicit columns first, then
INSERT INTO ... SELECT to populate it. The INSERT path doesn't depend
on schema inference from the SELECT and works reliably. Apply the same
treatment to fts_search_overview_mv, which has the same shape.

Fixes the archive_save_list_restore_roundtrip_smoke regression and the
seven other archive-finalize tests that hit this code path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
